reasoning: The resolution criterion strictly requires a formal declaration of war. Since the end of World War II, formal declarations of war between sovereign states have been exceptionally rare, largely due to international legal norms and the UN Charter. India and Pakistan have had numerous severe conflicts (like the 1999 Kargil War and the 2019 Pulwama/Balakot standoff) without formally declaring war. Even if major conflict were to occur in the remaining ~2 months of 2025, it is overwhelmingly likely it would remain an undeclared/proxy war or limited skirmish to prevent uncontrolled escalation, particularly given both nations are nuclear-armed. The tiny timeframe combined with the strictly literal "formal declaration" requirement pushes the probability to the minimum allowed floor.
